provenance.notes: Migrated for 0.20.0 schema: advisor pass produced via the mandatory advisor agent path before provenance was a required field.

diffCoverage.notes: Reconciliation: code merged in f343624; the previously-missing live phone-scan smoke is now captured (non-secret GUI log) and both required reviewers move to pass.

resolvedFindings.resolution: The live phone-scan smoke is captured (gui-live-2026-06-03.log) alongside attested build/lint/tests/structural/frontend.build checks; the QR modal (QrLoginScreen.svelte) exists; the evidence bundle is authored.
security-reviewer and architecture-reviewer pass decisions are now present and linked from the bundle.

findings.evidence: The captured live log shows the full flow: start_qr_login -> 6 cookies incl. zpw_sek extracted from the jar -> QR confirmed -> persisted account credential -> session established (account_id=1483678772784051123) -> ui success. The log is non-secret (cookie NAMES + account ids only). This GUI run is a superset of the contract's qr_login_live smoke.

credentials_from_qr mints a fresh imei per scan; recorded as the accepted known risk qr-imei-not-persisted-stable. secure-cred-store now persists + reuses the per-account credential (incl. imei) on restore, which satisfies the stability goal for restored accounts.
findings.fix: Non-blocking; revisit if upstream zca-rust emits a device imei (ADR-0004 defers this).

summary: qr-login is honestly done after reconciliation. The core flow (merged in f343624) keeps the credential triple in-core — the token-bearing GotLoginInfo is dropped before the webview (test-asserted), only non-secret QrLoginEvent/AccountProfile cross IPC, and zca-rust/reqwest stay confined to zalo. The single remaining gap that kept this needs-human — the live phone scan — is now done by the project owner against the running app, with a captured non-secret log proving session established + persisted credential. Build/lint/tests/structural/frontend.build are attested; both required reviewers pass. Pass.
